zh.lifeSummary: Joan Cecily Fleming de Everingham於1362年出生在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾拉可頓。她於1398年8月與Robert Waterton結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1434年在英國享年72歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭韋斯特 越可爾梅瑟利。

nameMeanings.name: Fleming
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: ethnic name for someone from Flanders, from Middle English fleming. The word reflects a Norman French form of Old French flamanc ‘Fleming’, from the stem flam- + the ancient Germanic suffix -ing. In the Middle Ages there was considerable commercial intercourse between England and the Netherlands, particularly in the wool trade, and many Flemish weavers and dyers settled in England. This surname is also common in south and east Scotland and in Ireland, where it is sometimes found in the Gaelicized form Pléamonn.
German: variant of   Flemming  , cognate with 1 above.

variants.name: Flemings
meanings: English: plural form of   Fleming  .
Americanized form of Flemish Vleminckx: patronymic from Vleminck, an ethnic name for someone from Flanders, Middle Dutch vleminc.

variants.name: Flanders
meanings: English: ethnic name from Middle English Fla(u)ndres ‘Flanders’, denoting someone from Flanders, a historical region covering parts of modern-day Belgium, France, and the Netherlands. Compare Fleming .

variants.name: Fleeman
meanings: English: variant of   Fleming  .

variants.name: Flemming
meanings: German: ethnic name for someone from Flanders, Middle High German vlaeminc. People from Flanders spread throughout Germany, as well as England, in the Middle Ages as clothmakers and dyers.
English: variant of   Fleming  .

variants.name: Flamenco
meanings: Spanish: ethnic name from flamenco ‘Fleming, Flemish person’ (see Fleming ).

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Everingham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (East Yorkshire): habitational name from Everingham (East Yorkshire), named in Old English Yferingaham ‘homestead (hām) of the people (-inga-) of Eofor’.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: Cecily
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular English form of   Cecilia .

variants.name: Cecilie
meanings: Medieval vernacular English form of   Cecilia .

variants.name: Cicely
meanings: Variant of   Cecily .  This was a common form of the name in the Middle Ages, and in the 20th century was well known as the name of the British actress and singer  Cicely Courtneidge  (  1893–1980 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Joan Cecily Fleming de Everingham was born in 1362, in Laxton, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. She married Robert Waterton in August 1398. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in 1434, in England, at the age of 72, and was buried in Methley, West Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Joan Cecily Fleming d’Everingham est née en 1362, à Laxton, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé Robert Waterton en août 1398. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1434, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 72 ans, et a été enterrée à Methley, West Yorkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Cecily Fleming de Everinghamは1362年にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアラクストンで生まれた。1398年8月にRobert Watertonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1434年にイギリスで72歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスウエスト ヨークシュアイアメスリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Cecily Fleming de Everingham(은)는 1362년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 라스턴에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1398년 8월 Robert Waterton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1434년 영국에서 72세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트 요르크실르 멧h리에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Joan Cecily Fleming de Everingham родилась в 1362 г. в местности лакстон, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Waterton август 1398 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1434 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 72 лет и была похоронена в местности метли, вест еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.
